stories.headline: Israel rejects ceasefire with Hezbollah before Lebanon talks next week
stories.summary: At least 10 people, including three emergency workers, killed in latest Israeli attacks on southern Lebanon.

stories.headline: At least seven Palestinians killed in Israeli attacks on Gaza
stories.summary: An early-morning strike hits a group of civilians in ​​Bureij camp as drones attack a tent in Khan Younis.

stories.headline: US and Iran begin highest-level talks in five decades
stories.summary: Delegations will discuss ending a war that has spread across the Gulf and stoked a global energy crisis

stories.headline: Ukraine and Russia exchange prisoners ahead of Orthodox Easter ceasefire
stories.summary: The warring sides exchanged 175 prisoners of war each in a mediation facilitated by the United Arab Emirates, officials from both nations said.

stories.headline: US and Iranian officials begin high-stakes peace talks in Pakistan to resolve war
stories.summary: Three party talks with the US, Iran and Pakistan have begun after a reduction in Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon and other preconditions being met, according to Iran's IRNA news agency.

stories.headline: Israeli drone attack kills Palestinians near Gaza mosque
stories.summary: An Israeli drone attack near a mosque in central Gaza has killed at least 6 people.

stories.headline: Russia-Ukraine Orthodox Easter ceasefire begins
stories.summary: Both sides have agreed to observe the temporary truce, as US-led diplomatic efforts to end the war continue to stall.

stories.headline: US-Iran direct talks on ending war under way in Pakistan
stories.summary: Discussions between Iran and US delegations begin following separate bilateral meetings with Pakistani prime minister.

stories.headline: Frontrunner for California governor denies sexual assault allegations
stories.summary: The Democratic congressman, a leading candidate in the race, has strongly denied the accusations saying he will fight with "facts".

stories.headline: EU airline industry warns of fuel shortages if Strait of Hormuz stays closed
stories.summary: Airports Council International (ACI) Europe said its members had "increasing concerns" about the availability of jet fuel.

stories.headline: The IMF Meetings Were Supposed to Be About Trade. Then Came the Iran War.
stories.summary: The IMF and World Bank meetings were supposed to be about trade and growth. Instead, the war in Iran has forced finance ministers and central bankers to confront a potential global economic crisis. Chrystia Freeland, former Deputy Prime Minister and Finance Minister of Canada, explains why this moment may mark the end of the U.S.-led international order that has kept the world stable since World War II. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Eric Swalwell Faces Sex Assault Accusation as He Runs for California Governor
stories.summary: Eric Swalwell, a Democratic congressman, denied the accounts published by CNN and The San Francisco Chronicle. Nancy Pelosi was among those who said he should withdraw from the California governor’s race.

stories.headline: Eric Swalwell, California governor candidate, accused of sexual assault by ex-staffer
stories.summary: Supporters of Rep. Eric Swalwell’s campaign withdrew support, including two congressmen who chaired his campaign and two senators who endorsed his bid. Swalwell denied the allegations.

stories.headline: A Panicked Race for Barrels Is Gripping the Global Oil Market
stories.summary: While investors focused on the fragile Iranian ceasefire this week, a desperate scramble for cargoes has been playing out in the oil market, as traders and refiners scour the globe for immediately available supplies.

stories.headline: Odd Lots: The Big Macro Force That’s Kept Stocks High (Podcast)
stories.summary: Stocks have gone up over the years because corporate earnings continue to grow. That part is straightforward. But in addition to rising stock prices, we’ve also seen rising stock market valuations. For years, investors have talked about stocks being unreasonably priced, and yet they haven’t reverted to historical norms. But perhaps there’s a good explanation for this, beyond just animal spirits. Jonathan Heathcote is an economist at the Minneapol

stories.headline: Video: JD Vance meets with Pakistani PM ahead of Iran talks
stories.summary: US officials have met Pakistani PM Shehbaz Sharif in Islamabad ahead of possible talks with Iran.

stories.summary: As Péter Magyar's opposition movement leads in the polls, tens of thousands of anti-Orbán supporters fill Heroes' Square in Budapest.

stories.headline: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote
stories.summary: Ismail Omar Guelleh, 78, has extended his already 27-year rule in a poll boycotted by the main opposition candidates.

stories.headline: Clients’ barrage of AI-generated queries risks pushing up lawyers’ fees
stories.summary: Firms may raise prices for fixed-fee contracts if clients keep sending flurries of emails and letters

stories.headline: Anthropic closes in on OpenAI as US business use surges
stories.summary: Divergence reflects company’s recent rapid growth owing to strong interest in its Claude Code products

stories.headline: Mortgage borrowers seek shorter-term deals as market volatility saps confidence
stories.summary: Flexibility is the watchword among UK borrowers amid signs of slowing activity in the housing market

stories.headline: Islamabad Peace Talks Latest
stories.summary: Pakistan is playing host to US and Iranian delegates in Islamabad. Pakistani officials are optimistic, but uncertaity remains around how much both parties will be willing to concede. Bloomberg's Pakistan Bureau Chief Faseeh Mangi reports from central Islamabad for Bloomberg This Weekend. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Bank of England Set to Discuss Anthropic’s Mythos With Banks
stories.summary: The Bank of England plans to discuss the impact of Anthropic PBC’s new AI model with financial institutions, as UK regulators join their peers in the US and elsewhere in raising alarms over the risks posed by the tool.

stories.headline: Europe Energy Traders Brace for 21-Hour Day as Volatility Surges
stories.summary: European gas and power markets, once a niche corner of global energy trading, are in for a major change next week: Hours will more than double to 21 from 10, ending the narrow daytime window that has been the norm for years.

stories.headline: The Big Macro Force That's Been Driving Stocks Higher for Years
stories.summary: How inequality has helped keep the stock market boom alive.

stories.headline: African Development Bank on Continent's Water Future
stories.summary: The African Development Bank says water investments on the continent have increased over the past decade — from upstream preparation of projects to desalination efforts, service delivery and wastewater treatment plants. But Mtchera Chirwa, the AfDB's water and sanitation department director, says gaps within the sector won't just be resolved with more capital injection. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: As Election Looms, Will Alito Retire, Giving Trump a New Supreme Court Pick?
stories.summary: Justice Samuel A. Alito Jr. is the subject of intense speculation about whether he will retire in the coming months and give President Trump a fourth nominee.

stories.headline: Zebras, wealth and power: Hungary’s election tests Orbán’s grip on power
stories.summary: Corruption scandals and a surging opposition have turned the vote into the biggest test yet for the long-serving populist leaderThe drone footage showed a sprawling residence in northern Hungary, complete with manicured gardens, a swimming pool and an underground garage. But it was what came next that captured much of the country’s imagination: zebras darting across the countryside. Continue reading...

stories.headline: Peru holds presidential election amid a decade of political tumult
stories.summary: In the span of a decade, Peru has seen nine presidents come and go. How has that affected this year's presidential race?

stories.headline: Police begin arrests at UK protest against Palestine Action ban
stories.summary: Hundreds of people gather in Trafalgar Square for demonstration, as elderly woman with cane is escorted away by police.

stories.headline: Libya approves first unified budget in more than a decade

stories.headline: UK to hold off on deal ceding Chagos Islands amid US opposition
stories.summary: A bill laying out plans to return the Indian Ocean archipelago, home to the US-UK Diego Garcia base, has been paused.

stories.headline: What’s at stake in Benin’s presidential election?
stories.summary: Finance minister is favourite to win on Sunday as incumbent Talon will step down after leading the country for 10 years.

stories.headline: JD Vance warns Iran not to 'play' US during planned talks in Pakistan

stories.summary: Over-reliance on imported gas and worries over Starmer’s future likely to stall pick-up in momentum, analysts say

stories.headline: Chimpanzees in Uganda locked in vicious 'civil war', say researchers
stories.summary: The once close-knit community of Ngogo chimpanzees have been at loggerheads for the last eight years.

stories.headline: Prince Harry sued for defamation by charity he co-founded
stories.summary: Legal action follows war of words with Sentebale chair after Duke of Sussex’s resignation as patronThe Duke of Sussex is being sued by Sentebale in the latest twist in the bitter fallout over the African charity he co-founded.The charity has lodged papers in London’s high court over defamation claims naming Prince Harry and the former Sentebale trustee Mark Dyer as defendants. Continue reading...

stories.headline: Taiwan opposition leader meets Xi Jinping in Beijing
stories.summary: Cheng Li-wun is the Kuomintang's first sitting leader to visit China in a decade.

stories.headline: Burned Before, Democrats Abruptly Revive the Idea of Impeaching Trump
stories.summary: Democrats in Congress have assiduously avoided talk of a third impeachment of President Trump, concerned that it would distract from their midterm campaign message. That tide seems to have turned.

stories.headline: Officials Release Design for 250-Foot Arch in Washington, as Trump Seeks Another Imprint
stories.summary: The president has proposed the arch, which would rise on a Washington roundabout across from the Lincoln Memorial, as a way to celebrate America’s 250th anniversary.

stories.headline: Eliot Engel, a prominent voice on foreign affairs in the House, dies at 79
stories.summary: The New York Democrat helped lead the first impeachment inquiry into President Donald Trump. He served for 16 terms before losing his reelection bid in 2020.

stories.headline: Irish government prepares fuel support deal after blockade pressure
stories.summary: Travel across parts of the Republic of Ireland was affected for the fourth day in a row as vehicles, including tractors, blocked roads.

stories.headline: Trump Moves Top Aide James Blair to Political Operation Ahead of Midterms
stories.summary: Mr. Blair will “lead the charge from the outside,” the president said on social media. The move comes as Republicans are bracing for a potentially difficult November election.

stories.headline: Has US achieved its war objectives in Iran?
stories.summary: Key US objectives at the start of the war were to stop Iran getting a nuclear weapon and degrading its arsenal.

stories.headline: Lebanon thought there was a ceasefire - then Israel unleashed deadly blitz

stories.headline: Argentina passes bill loosening protection of its glaciers
stories.summary: The new bill will make it easier for provincial governments to allow mining projects in glacier regions.

stories.headline: DOJ presses for its own search of Post reporter’s phone, laptops
stories.summary: Federal prosecutors want to overturn a magistrate judge’s decision to review reporter Hannah Natanson’s seized devices on behalf of the government and turn over information relevant to a leak investigation.

stories.headline: At least 182 killed across Lebanon in large wave of Israeli strikes

stories.headline: Vietnam's leader To Lam strengthens power in unanimous assembly vote
